name: revocation: a revoked device's next WS frame gets error{code:'revoked'} and closes 4001; its token also 401s over HTTP
description: Deleting a device row (what `matron-admin device revoke` does) doesn't tear the socket down immediately — enforcement is next-frame: the connection's next inbound frame after revocation gets {kind:'control', op:'error', code:'revoked'} and the server closes with code 4001. The same token is rejected by every HTTP endpoint on the very next request (token-hash lookup per request, no separate revocation list). No wall-clock waits: the pre-revocation `send`'s journal echo both proves normal frames work before revocation AND is the causal barrier guaranteeing every earlier frame was processed before the row is deleted (per-connection FIFO); admin_revoke_device itself is synchronous in the runner.
